1	\chapter{Section graded ring infrastructure: tensor powers and graded sections}
2	\label{chap:Picard_SectionGradedRing}
3	
4	\providecommand{\PshMod}{\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}}
5	\providecommand{\ShMod}{\mathrm{SheafOfModules}}
6	\providecommand{\Modules}{\mathrm{Modules}}
7	
8	The Hilbert-polynomial route of \cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme} represents the
9	degree-\(m\) graded component of a fibre by the global sections
10	\(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) of a twist of a coherent
11	sheaf by a tensor power of a line bundle, and it organises these components into
12	a graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s) = \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
13	(\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) and a graded module
14	\(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s) = \bigoplus_{m \ge 0}
15	\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
16	(\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}). Three pieces of that picture have no direct
17	Mathlib counterpart and must be built here, each in its own section:
18	
19	\begin{enumerate}
20	  \item \textbf{Tensor powers of a sheaf of modules}
21	    (\cref{sec:sgr_tensor_powers}). Mathlib equips the category
22	    \(\PshMod\) of \emph{presheaves} of modules over a presheaf of commutative
23	    rings with a symmetric monoidal structure
24	    (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) and provides the
25	    sheafification functor \(\PshMod \to \ShMod\)
26	    (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}); but the category
27	    \(\ShMod\) of \emph{sheaves} of modules carries \emph{no} monoidal
28	    structure. The tensor product of sheaves of modules
29	    \(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\) must therefore be built
30	    as the sheafification of the pointwise tensor presheaf, and the iterated
31	    tensor power \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m}\) recursively from it.
32	  \item \textbf{Lax-monoidal global sections} (\cref{sec:sgr_lax_sections}).
33	    The global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is only \emph{lax} monoidal: from the
34	    pointwise-strict monoidality of the presheaf tensor product and the
35	    sheafification unit one obtains a natural multiplication
36	    \(\Gamma(\mathcal{F}) \otimes_{\Gamma(\mathcal{O}_X)} \Gamma(\mathcal{G})
37	    \to \Gamma(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})\), together with
38	    the associativity and unit coherence those products must satisfy.
39	  \item \textbf{Graded-ring / graded-module assembly}
40	    (\cref{sec:sgr_graded_assembly}). The section multiplications and the
41	    tensor-power comparison isomorphisms assemble, through Mathlib's
42	    external-direct-sum graded API (\cref{lem:directSum_gcommSemiring_mathlib},
43	    \cref{lem:directSum_gmodule_mathlib}), into the graded \emph{ring} structure
44	    on \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\) and the graded
45	    \emph{module} structure on
46	    \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\).
47	\end{enumerate}
48	
49	\section{Tensor powers of a sheaf of modules}
50	\label{sec:sgr_tensor_powers}
51	
52	We work over a fixed scheme \(X\) (in the application \(X = X_s\), a fibre over a
53	field \(\kappa(s)\)). Its category of sheaves of modules is
54	\(X.\Modules = \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\), with global-sections notation
55	\(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{M}) = \mathcal{M}(X)\) for the value of the underlying
56	presheaf on the top open. The structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) is a sheaf of
57	commutative rings, so its underlying presheaf factors through the category of
58	commutative rings; this is the hypothesis under which Mathlib's presheaf-level
59	monoidal structure applies.
60	
61	\begin{lemma}[Monoidal structure on presheaves of modules]
62	  \label{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
63	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}
64	  \mathlibok
65	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
66	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal}).}
67	  Let \(R : \mathcal{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{CommRingCat}\) be a presheaf of
68	  commutative rings. Then the category \(\PshMod(R)\) of presheaves of modules
69	  over \(R\) carries a symmetric monoidal structure whose tensor product is
70	  computed objectwise: for presheaves of modules \(\mathcal{M}_1, \mathcal{M}_2\)
71	  and an object \(U\),
72	  \[
73	    (\mathcal{M}_1 \otimes \mathcal{M}_2)(U)
74	      \;=\; \mathcal{M}_1(U) \otimes_{R(U)} \mathcal{M}_2(U),
75	  \]
76	  with restriction maps the tensor products of the restriction maps. The unit is
77	  the presheaf \(R\) viewed as a module over itself, and the associator,
78	  unitors, and braiding are induced objectwise from those of
79	  \(\mathrm{ModuleCat}\). In particular evaluation at a fixed object \(U\) is a
80	  \emph{strict} monoidal functor
81	  \(\PshMod(R) \to \mathrm{ModuleCat}(R(U))\).
82	\end{lemma}
83	
84	\begin{lemma}[Sheafification of presheaves of modules]
85	  \label{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
86	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}
87	  \mathlibok
88	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
89	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification}).}
90	  Let \(R\) be a sheaf of (commutative) rings on a site, with underlying
91	  presheaf \(R_0\). There is a sheafification functor
92	  \[
93	    (-)^{\#} \;:\; \PshMod(R_0) \longrightarrow \ShMod(R),
94	  \]
95	  left adjoint to the forgetful functor, together with the unit
96	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{M}_0} : \mathcal{M}_0 \to (\mathcal{M}_0)^{\#}\) of the
97	  adjunction. On underlying sheaves of abelian groups it agrees with the
98	  ordinary sheafification, and it sends a presheaf of \(R_0\)-modules to the
99	  associated sheaf of \(R\)-modules.
100	\end{lemma}
101	
102	\begin{lemma}[The structure sheaf as the unit module]
103	  \label{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
104	  \lean{SheafOfModules.unit}
105	  \mathlibok
106	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
107	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf}).}
108	  For a sheaf of rings \(R\) (in the application \(R = \mathcal{O}_X\)) the
109	  structure sheaf, viewed as a module over itself, is a distinguished object
110	  \(\mathbf{1} = \mathcal{O}_X \in \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\). It is the value at
111	  the zeroth tensor power, \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} = \mathcal{O}_X\), and its
112	  global sections \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) form the degree-zero component of
113	  the section graded ring.
114	\end{lemma}
115	
116	\begin{definition}\leanok
117	[Presheaf-of-modules category in monoidal form]
118	  \label{def:monoidalPresheaf}
119	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.MonoidalPresheaf}
120	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
121	  For a scheme \(X\), write \(\PshMod_{\otimes}(X)\) for the category of presheaves
122	  of modules over the underlying presheaf of (plain) rings of \(\mathcal{O}_X\),
123	  obtained by composing the structure sheaf with the forgetful functor
124	  \(\mathrm{CommRingCat} \to \mathrm{RingCat}\). This is definitionally
125	  \(X.\PshMod\), but presented in the exact form for which Mathlib equips it with
126	  the symmetric monoidal structure of \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib};
127	  it is the carrier on which the objectwise tensor product of
128	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} is taken.
129	\end{definition}
130	
131	\begin{definition}\leanok
132	[Scheme-level module sheafification functor]
133	  \label{def:schemeModuleSheafification}
134	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification}
135	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
136	  For a scheme \(X\), the \emph{module sheafification functor}
137	  \((-)^{\#} : X.\PshMod \to X.\Modules\) is Mathlib's sheafification functor
138	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) specialised to the identity
139	  morphism of the underlying presheaf of rings of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (a locally
140	  bijective map). Applying it to the objectwise tensor presheaf is what defines the
141	  sheaf tensor product \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}.
142	\end{definition}
143	
144	\begin{definition}\leanok
145	[Unit module of a scheme]
146	  \label{def:unitModule}
147	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.unitModule}
148	  \uses{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
149	  For a scheme \(X\), the \emph{unit module} \(\mathbf{1}_X \in X.\Modules\) is the
150	  structure sheaf viewed as a module over itself, i.e.\ Mathlib's
151	  \(\mathrm{SheafOfModules.unit}\) (\cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}) for
152	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\). It is the value of the zeroth tensor power,
153	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} = \mathbf{1}_X\) (\cref{def:sheafTensorPow}).
154	\end{definition}
155	
156	\begin{definition}\leanok
157	[Tensor product of sheaves of modules]
158	  \label{def:sheafTensorObj}
159	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
160	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
161	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
162	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", \S "Tensor product" (Tag
163	  % 01CA), prose definition (read from references/stacks-modules.tex,
164	  % L2282-L2297).
165	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
166	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space and let
167	  %  $\mathcal{F}$ and $\mathcal{G}$ be $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
168	  %  We define first the {\it tensor product presheaf}
169	  %  $$
170	  %  \mathcal{F} \otimes_{p, \mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
171	  %  $$
172	  %  as the rule which assigns to $U \subset X$ open the
173	  %  $\mathcal{O}_X(U)$-module
174	  %  $\mathcal{F}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{G}(U)$.
175	  %  Having defined this we define the {\it tensor product sheaf}
176	  %  as the sheafification of the above:
177	  %  $$
178	  %  \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
179	  %  =
180	  %  (\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p, \mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})^\#
181	  %  $$"
182	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", Tag 01CA (tensor
183	  product).}
184	  Let \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) be sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Their
185	  \emph{tensor product} is the sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
186	  \[
187	    \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
188	      \;=\; \bigl(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\bigr)^{\#},
189	  \]
190	  the sheafification (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) of the
191	  tensor product presheaf \(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\),
192	  whose value on an open \(U\) is the \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module
193	  \(\mathcal{F}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{G}(U)\). Concretely, the
194	  tensor product presheaf is the Mathlib presheaf-level monoidal product
195	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) of the underlying presheaves of
196	  modules of \(\mathcal{F}\) and \(\mathcal{G}\), and
197	  \(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\) is its sheafification. This
198	  construction is symmetric and associative because the presheaf tensor product
199	  is, and sheafification preserves the coherence isomorphisms.
200	\end{definition}
201	
202	\begin{definition}\leanok
203	[Tensor power of a line bundle]
204	  \label{def:sheafTensorPow}
205	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow}
206	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:unitModule, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
207	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", \S "Invertible modules",
208	  % `definition-powers' (Tag 01CU) (read from references/stacks-modules.tex,
209	  % L4220-L4228).
210	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
211	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. Given an invertible sheaf
212	  %  $\mathcal{L}$ on $X$ and $n \in \mathbf{Z}$ we define the
213	  %  $n$th {\it tensor power} $\mathcal{L}^{\otimes n}$ of $\mathcal{L}$
214	  %  as the image of $\mathcal{O}_X$ under applying the equivalence
215	  %  $\mathcal{F} \mapsto\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{L}$
216	  %  exactly $n$ times."
217	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", Tag 01CU (tensor
218	  powers).}
219	  Let \(\mathcal{L}\) be a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (in the application
220	  an invertible sheaf / line bundle \(L_s\)). For \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) the
221	  \emph{\(m\)th tensor power} \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m}\) is defined by recursion
222	  on \(m\):
223	  \[
224	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} = \mathcal{O}_X
225	      \quad(\text{the unit module, \cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}}),
226	    \qquad
227	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)}
228	      = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{L}
229	        \quad(\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}).
230	  \]
231	  We restrict to non-negative powers, which is all the section graded ring uses;
232	  the Stacks definition extends \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes n}\) to all
233	  \(n \in \mathbf{Z}\) using invertibility, but negative powers are not needed
234	  here. For \(\mathcal{L}\) invertible each \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m}\) is again
235	  invertible.
236	\end{definition}
237	
238	\begin{lemma}\leanok
239	[Zeroth tensor power is the unit]
240	  \label{lem:tensorPow_zero}
241	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow_zero}
242	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:unitModule}
243	  For a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{L}\) on \(X\) one has
244	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} = \mathbf{1}_X\), the unit module
245	  (\cref{def:unitModule}); this is the base clause of the recursive definition
246	  \cref{def:sheafTensorPow}, holding by definition.
247	\end{lemma}
248	
249	\begin{lemma}\leanok
250	[Tensor-power recursion step]
251	  \label{lem:tensorPow_succ}
252	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow_succ}
253	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj}
254	  For a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{L}\) on \(X\) and \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) one has
255	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
256	  \mathcal{L}\) (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}); this is the successor clause of
257	  \cref{def:sheafTensorPow}, holding by definition.
258	\end{lemma}
259	
260	\begin{definition}\leanok
261	[Twist of a coherent sheaf by a tensor power]
262	  \label{def:sheafModuleTwist}
263	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow}
264	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:sheafTensorPow}
265	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (in the application
266	  a coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}_s\)) and \(\mathcal{L}\) a line bundle. For
267	  \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) the \emph{\(m\)-twist} of \(\mathcal{F}\) by
268	  \(\mathcal{L}\) is
269	  \[
270	    \mathcal{F}(m) \;:=\;
271	      \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m}
272	      \qquad(\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}, \cref{def:sheafTensorPow}),
273	  \]
274	  the degree-\(m\) carrier of the section graded module. By construction
275	  \(\mathcal{F}(0) = \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{O}_X \cong
276	  \mathcal{F}\).
277	\end{definition}
278	
279	\begin{lemma}\leanok
280	[Zeroth twist]
281	  \label{lem:moduleTensorPow_zero}
282	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow_zero}
283	  \uses{def:sheafModuleTwist, def:sheafTensorObj, def:unitModule,
284	    def:tensorObjRightUnitor}
285	  For sheaves of modules \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{L}\) on \(X\) one has
286	  \(\mathcal{F}(0) = \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathbf{1}_X\)
287	  (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}, \cref{def:unitModule}); this is the base clause of
288	  \cref{def:sheafModuleTwist}, holding by definition, and is canonically
289	  isomorphic to \(\mathcal{F}\) via the right unitor
290	  (\cref{def:tensorObjRightUnitor}).
291	\end{lemma}
292	
293	The next four isomorphisms are the parts of the (would-be) symmetric monoidal
294	structure on \(X.\Modules\) that descend through sheafification from
295	\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib} using only \emph{functoriality} of the
296	sheafification functor (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) and, for the
297	unitors, the reflective counit isomorphism --- no strong monoidality of
298	sheafification is required. They are the structural inputs of the tensor-power
299	comparison \cref{lem:sheafTensorPow_add}.
300	
301	\begin{definition}\leanok
302	[Counit isomorphism of module sheafification]
303	  \label{def:sheafificationCounitIso}
304	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafificationCounitIso}
305	  \uses{def:schemeModuleSheafification, lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
306	  For a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{G}\) on \(X\) the counit of the sheafification
307	  adjunction (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib},
308	  \cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) gives a canonical isomorphism
309	  \((\mathcal{G})^{\#} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{G}\) of sheaves of modules,
310	  invertible because the right adjoint forgetful functor to presheaves of modules
311	  is fully faithful.
312	\end{definition}
313	
314	\begin{definition}\leanok
315	[Left unitor of the sheaf tensor product]
316	  \label{def:tensorObjUnitIso}
317	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjUnitIso}
318	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:unitModule, def:sheafificationCounitIso,
319	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
320	  For a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{G}\) on \(X\) there is a canonical left-unitor
321	  isomorphism \(\mathbf{1}_X \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
322	  \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{G}\) (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj},
323	  \cref{def:unitModule}), obtained by sheafifying the presheaf left unitor of
324	  \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib} and composing with the counit
325	  isomorphism \cref{def:sheafificationCounitIso}. It is the base case
326	  (\(m = 0\)) of \cref{lem:sheafTensorPow_add}.
327	\end{definition}
328	
329	\begin{definition}\leanok
330	[Right unitor of the sheaf tensor product]
331	  \label{def:tensorObjRightUnitor}
332	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjRightUnitor}
333	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:unitModule, def:sheafificationCounitIso,
334	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
335	  For a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{G}\) on \(X\) there is a canonical right-unitor
336	  isomorphism \(\mathcal{G} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathbf{1}_X
337	  \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{G}\) (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj},
338	  \cref{def:unitModule}), the sheafification of the presheaf right unitor
339	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) composed with the counit
340	  isomorphism \cref{def:sheafificationCounitIso}.
341	\end{definition}
342	
343	\begin{definition}\leanok
344	[Braiding of the sheaf tensor product]
345	  \label{def:tensorBraiding}
346	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorBraiding}
347	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:schemeModuleSheafification,
348	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
349	  For sheaves of modules \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) on \(X\) there is a canonical
350	  braiding isomorphism \(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
351	  \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{G} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{F}\)
352	  (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}), obtained by sheafifying
353	  (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) the symmetric braiding of the presheaf
354	  monoidal structure (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}). It is the
355	  symmetry used in the inductive step of \cref{lem:sheafTensorPow_add}.
356	\end{definition}
357	
358	The strong-monoidality comparison \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
359	below is assembled from three reduction lemmas --- a localization criterion, the
360	local-isomorphism property of the unit, and their corollary --- together with the
361	coequalizer presentation of the relative module-presheaf tensor product, the single
362	Mathlib-absent brick on which the comparison rests.
363	
364	\begin{lemma}\leanok
365	[Localization criterion for module sheafification]
366	  \label{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff}
367	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_sheafification_map_iff}
368	  \uses{def:schemeModuleSheafification, lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
369	  For a morphism \(f : \mathcal{P} \to \mathcal{Q}\) of presheaves of
370	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the sheafification \((f)^{\#}\)
371	  (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules if
372	  and only if the underlying morphism of abelian-group presheaves lies in the
373	  weak-equivalence class \(J.W\) of the opens topology \(J\) on \(X\) (i.e.\ is a
374	  local isomorphism of abelian-group presheaves).
375	\end{lemma}
376	
377	\begin{proof}
378	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
379	  \leanok
380	  Module sheafification (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) is the localization
381	  functor at the class \(W\) of morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying
382	  abelian-presheaf morphism lies in \(J.W\), i.e.\ the inverse image of \(J.W\) along
383	  the forgetful functor. The localization criterion for a reflective adjunction
384	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) then gives the stated
385	  equivalence.
386	\end{proof}
387	
388	\begin{lemma}\leanok
389	[The sheafification unit is an abelian local isomorphism]
390	  \label{lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit}
391	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit}
392	  \uses{def:schemeModuleSheafification, lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
393	  For a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{P}\) the underlying
394	  abelian-group presheaf morphism of the sheafification unit
395	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} : \mathcal{P} \to (\mathcal{P})^{\#}\)
396	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) is the abelian sheafification
397	  unit \(\mathcal{P} \to a(\mathcal{P})\); it therefore lies in the weak-equivalence
398	  class \(J.W\) of the opens topology.
399	\end{lemma}
400	
401	\begin{proof}
402	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
403	  \leanok
404	  The forgetful functor to abelian-group presheaves carries the module
405	  sheafification unit to the unit of the abelian associated-sheaf adjunction, which
406	  is a local isomorphism, hence a member of \(J.W\).
407	\end{proof}
408	
409	\begin{lemma}\leanok
410	[Sheafification inverts the localization unit]
411	  \label{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_unit}
412	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_sheafification_map_unit}
413	  \uses{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit}
414	  For a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{P}\) the sheafification
415	  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})^{\#}\) of the localization unit is an isomorphism of sheaves
416	  of modules. This is the un-whiskered (\(m = 0\)) special case of
417	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}.
418	\end{lemma}
419	
420	\begin{proof}
421	  \uses{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit}
422	  \leanok
423	  Feed the abelian local-isomorphism statement
424	  \cref{lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit} through the localization criterion
425	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff}.
426	\end{proof}
427	
428	\begin{definition}\leanok
429	[Step-1 objectwise \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor presheaf]
430	  \label{def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}
431	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.relTensorDomainPresheaf}
432	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
433	  For presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) the
434	  assignment \(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)\),

509	\begin{lemma}[A \(\mathbb{Z}\)-whiskered stalkwise isomorphism is a local isomorphism]
510	  \label{lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso}
511	  % NOTE: Lean decl name pending --- no `\lean{}` pin yet; add when the Lean
512	  % declaration for this whiskered-stalkwise-iso lemma lands.
513	  \uses{lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit, def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}
514	  Let \(f : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be a morphism of abelian-group
515	  presheaves on the space \(X\) that is a \emph{stalkwise isomorphism} (each
516	  stalk map \(f_x\) is an isomorphism), and let \(\mathcal{C}\) be a further
517	  abelian-group presheaf. Then the whiskered map
518	  \(f \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{C}}\) of \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor
519	  presheaves is again a stalkwise isomorphism, hence a local isomorphism (a
520	  member of \(J.W\) for the opens topology \(J\)).
521	\end{lemma}
522	
523	\begin{proof}
524	  \uses{def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}
525	  The stalk functor \(\mathcal{A} \mapsto \mathcal{A}_x\) at a point \(x\) is a
526	  filtered colimit over the neighbourhoods of \(x\), and \(\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}\)
527	  commutes with filtered colimits, so
528	  \((\mathcal{A} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{C})_x \cong
529	  \mathcal{A}_x \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{C}_x\) naturally in \(\mathcal{A}\).
530	  Under this identification the stalk of \(f \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\) is
531	  \(f_x \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\), an isomorphism since \(f_x\) is.
532	  Thus \(f \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\) is a stalkwise isomorphism; a
533	  stalkwise isomorphism of abelian-group presheaves on a space is exactly a local
534	  isomorphism, i.e.\ lies in \(J.W\).
535	\end{proof}

537	\begin{lemma}[Abelian universal property of the relative tensor product]
538	  \label{lem:tensorProduct_liftAddHom_mathlib}
539	  \lean{TensorProduct.liftAddHom}
540	  \mathlibok
541	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Basic}).}
542	  Let \(S\) be a commutative ring and \(M, N, P\) be \(S\)-modules (or, more
543	  generally, the abelian-group statement: \(M, N\) modules and \(P\) an additive
544	  commutative group). An additive-bilinear map
545	  \(f : M \to (N \to P)\) that is \emph{\(S\)-balanced}, i.e.\ satisfies
546	  \(f(s \cdot m)(n) = f(m)(s \cdot n)\) for all \(s \in S\), factors uniquely
547	  through the relative tensor product as an additive group homomorphism
548	  \(\widetilde{f} : M \otimes_S N \to P\) with
549	  \(\widetilde{f}(m \otimes_S n) = f(m)(n)\). This is the abelian-group universal
550	  property of \(M \otimes_S N\): it is the quotient of \(M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N\)
551	  by the \(S\)-balancing relations.
552	\end{lemma}

554	\begin{lemma}[The relative tensor of modules is a coequalizer of abelian groups]
555	  \label{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer}
556	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork,
557	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actN,
558	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actM,
559	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap,
560	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap,
561	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL,
562	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL_surjective,
563	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL_comp_act,
564	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.aL,
565	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.aR,
566	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.piMor,
567	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.piMor_epi,
568	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.coeq_condition,
569	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.cofork,
570	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.descHom,
571	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.descMor,
572	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.descFac,
573	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul,
574	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap_tmul,
575	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL_tmul,
576	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.piMor_apply,
577	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.descHom_tmul}
578	  \uses{lem:tensorProduct_liftAddHom_mathlib}
579	  Let \(S\) be a commutative ring and \(M, N\) be \(S\)-modules. The abelian group
580	  \(M \otimes_S N\) is the coequalizer, \emph{in the category
581	  \(\mathrm{AddCommGrp}\) of abelian groups}, of the two parallel
582	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-linear \(S\)-action maps
583	  \[
584	    a_L,\, a_R :
585	    M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} (S \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N)
586	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
587	    M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N,
588	  \]
589	  where the \emph{left} action map sends \(m \otimes (s \otimes n) \mapsto
590	  (s \cdot m) \otimes n\) and the \emph{right} action map sends
591	  \(m \otimes (s \otimes n) \mapsto m \otimes (s \cdot n)\). The coforking map is
592	  the canonical projection \(\pi : M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N \to M \otimes_S N\),
593	  \(m \otimes n \mapsto m \otimes_S n\).
594	\end{lemma}
595	
596	\begin{proof}
597	  \uses{lem:tensorProduct_liftAddHom_mathlib}
598	  Write \(a_N : S \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N \to N\) and
599	  \(a_M : M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} S \to M\) for the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-linear maps
600	  induced by the scalar action (\(s \otimes n \mapsto s \cdot n\),
601	  \(m \otimes s \mapsto s \cdot m\)); the right action map is
602	  \(a_R = \mathrm{id}_M \otimes a_N\) and the left action map is
603	  \(a_L = (a_M \otimes \mathrm{id}_N) \circ \mathrm{assoc}^{-1}\), where
604	  \(\mathrm{assoc}\) is the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor associator. The projection
605	  \(\pi\) is the additive homomorphism produced by the abelian universal property
606	  \cref{lem:tensorProduct_liftAddHom_mathlib} from the \(S\)-balanced bilinear map
607	  \((m, n) \mapsto m \otimes_S n\).
608	
609	  \emph{Cofork condition.} On an elementary tensor
610	  \(m \otimes (s \otimes n)\) we have
611	  \(\pi(a_L(m \otimes s \otimes n)) = (s \cdot m) \otimes_S n
612	  = m \otimes_S (s \cdot n) = \pi(a_R(m \otimes s \otimes n))\), the middle
613	  equality being the defining \(S\)-balancing relation of \(M \otimes_S N\); since
614	  elementary tensors generate, \(\pi \circ a_L = \pi \circ a_R\).
615	
616	  \emph{Universal property.} Given any abelian group \(P\) and a homomorphism
617	  \(g : M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N \to P\) with \(g \circ a_L = g \circ a_R\), the
618	  bilinear map \((m, n) \mapsto g(m \otimes n)\) is \(S\)-balanced (that is exactly
619	  the cofork condition read on elementary tensors), so by
620	  \cref{lem:tensorProduct_liftAddHom_mathlib} it factors as a unique
621	  \(\widetilde{g} : M \otimes_S N \to P\) with \(\widetilde{g} \circ \pi = g\).
622	  Existence is this descended map; uniqueness follows because \(\pi\) is
623	  surjective, hence an epimorphism of abelian groups, so \(\widetilde{g} \circ \pi
624	  = g\) determines \(\widetilde{g}\). Packaging existence and uniqueness gives the
625	  colimit cofork.
626	
627	\end{proof}

629	\begin{lemma}[Relative tensor of module presheaves as a coequalizer]
630	  \label{lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer}
631	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.relativeTensorCoequalizerIso}
632	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
633	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer,
634	    lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib,
635	    lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib}
636	  Let \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) be presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The
637	  underlying abelian-group presheaf of their relative tensor product
638	  \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\)
639	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) is naturally the coequalizer of the
640	  parallel pair
641	  \[
642	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
643	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
644	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
645	  \]
646	  of abelian-group presheaves, where the first map acts the ring factor on the left,
647	  \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto (r \cdot p) \otimes q\), and the second on the
648	  right, \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto p \otimes (r \cdot q)\). The identification
649	  is objectwise and natural in \(U\).
650	\end{lemma}
651	
652	\begin{proof}
653	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
654	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer,
655	    lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib,
656	    lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib}
657	  The argument promotes the objectwise coequalizer of
658	  \cref{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer} to the functor category, in
659	  three steps.
660	
661	  \textbf{1. Objectwise.} For each open \(U\), apply
662	  \cref{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer} with \(S = \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
663	  \(M = \mathcal{P}(U)\), \(N = \mathcal{Q}(U)\): the abelian group
664	  \(\mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) is the coequalizer
665	  in \(\mathrm{AddCommGrp}\) of the two \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-action maps
666	  \[
667	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X(U)
668	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)
669	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
670	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U),
671	  \]
672	  with cofork map the projection \(\pi_U\).
673	
674	  \textbf{2. Naturality / promotion.} The three objectwise families assemble into
675	  morphisms of abelian-group presheaves
676	  \[
677	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X
678	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
679	    \;\overset{a_L,\,a_R}{\rightrightarrows}\;
680	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
681	    \;\overset{\pi}{\longrightarrow}\;
682	    \bigl(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\bigr),
683	  \]
684	  i.e.\ natural transformations of functors
685	  \(\mathcal{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{AddCommGrp}\). Naturality of
686	  \(a_L, a_R, \pi\) in \(U\) is the compatibility of the module action with the
687	  restriction maps of \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{O}_X, \mathcal{Q}\): the restriction
688	  of \(s \cdot m\) is \((s|_V) \cdot (m|_V)\), and likewise for the projection,
689	  because the relative tensor is functorial in \(U\). Colimits in a functor
690	  category are computed objectwise --- by
691	  \cref{lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib} a cocone is a colimit as
692	  soon as every evaluation \((\mathrm{ev}_U)\) of it is --- so the objectwise
693	  coequalizers of step 1 assemble into a coequalizer of the displayed parallel
694	  pair in \(\mathcal{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{AddCommGrp}\).
695	
696	  \textbf{3. Identification of the apex.} The apex presheaf
697	  \(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) is, by the
698	  objectwise tensor formula of the presheaf monoidal structure
699	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib}: \((\mathcal{M}_1 \otimes_p
700	  \mathcal{M}_2)(U) = \mathcal{M}_1(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}
701	  \mathcal{M}_2(U)\)), exactly the underlying abelian-group presheaf of the
702	  relative tensor product \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\)
703	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}). Transporting the colimit of step 2
704	  along this identification exhibits
705	  \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\), as an abelian-group
706	  presheaf, as the coequalizer of the parallel pair, naturally in \(U\).
707	\end{proof}

709	\begin{lemma}[Objectwise colimits in a functor category]
710	  \label{lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib}
711	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits}
712	  \mathlibok
713	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
714	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FunctorCategory.Basic}).}
715	  Let \(\mathcal{D}\) be a category and \(\mathcal{A}\) a category in which the
716	  relevant colimits exist. A cocone over a diagram
717	  \(F : \mathcal{J} \to (\mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{A})\) in the functor category is
718	  a colimit cocone as soon as, for every object \(U\) of \(\mathcal{D}\), its image
719	  under evaluation \(\mathrm{ev}_U : (\mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{A}) \to \mathcal{A}\)
720	  is a colimit cocone in \(\mathcal{A}\). Equivalently, colimits in a functor
721	  category are computed objectwise.
722	\end{lemma}

493	presheaves of modules over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the
494	`ℤ`-tensors of the underlying restriction maps (the middle factor restricting via the ring

496	presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`); objectwise it is the triple tensor on which

@[simp] private lemma objRestrict_apply (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (x : ↥(P.obj U)) :

input.new_string:
@[simp] private lemma objRestrict_apply (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (x : ↥(P.obj U)) :
    objRestrict P f x = (P.presheaf.map f).hom x := rfl

/-- Identity law for the syntactic-carrier restriction: `objRestrict P (𝟙 U) = id`. -/
private lemma objRestrict_id (P : X.PresheafOfModules) (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) :
    objRestrict P (𝟙 U) = LinearMap.id := by
  ext x
  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe,
    id_eq]

/-- Composition law for the syntactic-carrier restriction:
`objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g) ∘ (objRestrict P f)`. -/
private lemma objRestrict_comp (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
    {U V W : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (g : V ⟶ W) :
    objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g).comp (objRestrict P f) := by
  ext x
  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
    LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply]

private lemma objRestrict_id (P : X.PresheafOfModules) (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) :
    objRestrict P (𝟙 U) = LinearMap.id := by
  ext x
  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe,
    id_eq]

/-- Composition law for the syntactic-carrier restriction:
`objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g) ∘ (objRestrict P f)`. -/
private lemma objRestrict_comp (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
    {U V W : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (g : V ⟶ W) :
    objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g).comp (objRestrict P f) := by
  ext x
  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
    LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply]

477	over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the `ℤ`-tensors of the
478	two underlying restriction maps.  This is the codomain (apex-adjacent) presheaf of the cofork
479	in the presheaf promotion of `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork`; it is the concrete
480	Step-1 brick of the presheaf-level coequalizer iso `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`
481	(`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).  Project-local: no objectwise `ℤ`-tensor of
482	abelian-group presheaves is provided by Mathlib (`AddCommGrpCat` carries no monoidal

510	`ℤ`-tensors of the underlying restriction maps (the middle factor restricting via the ring
511	restriction map of `𝒪_X`).  This is the **domain** row of the relative-tensor coequalizer
512	presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`); objectwise it is the triple tensor on which
513	the two `R(U)`-action maps `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`/`actRmap` act.  Project-local: no
514	objectwise `ℤ`-tensor of abelian-group presheaves is provided by Mathlib. -/

module

prelude
public import Lean.Elab.MutualDef
public import Lean.Elab.MutualInductive
import Lean.Compiler.ExternAttr

public section
namespace Lean.Elab.Command

open Meta

private def ensureValidNamespace (name : Name) : MacroM Unit := do
  match name with
  | .str p s =>
    if s == "_root_" then
      Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, `_root_` is a reserved namespace"
    ensureValidNamespace p
  | .num .. => Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, it must not contain numeric parts"
  | .anonymous => return ()

private def setDeclIdName (declId : Syntax) (nameNew : Name) : Syntax :=
  let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore declId
  -- We should not update the name of `def _root_.` declarations
  assert! !(`_root_).isPrefixOf id
  let idStx := mkIdent nameNew |>.raw.setInfo declId.getHeadInfo
  if declId.isIdent then
    idStx
  else
    declId.setArg 0 idStx

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is a `Command.declaration`, and it is a declaration that always has a name. -/
private def isNamedDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  if !stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration then
    false
  else
    let decl := stx[1]
    let k := decl.getKind
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.abbrev ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.definition ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.theorem ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.opaque ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.axiom ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.inductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.structure

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is an `instance` declaration command -/
private def isInstanceDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration &&
  stx[1].getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.instance

/-- Return `some name` if `stx` is a declaration named `name` -/
private def getDeclName? (stx : Syntax) : Option Name := do
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore stx[1][1]
    some id
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    let optDeclId := stx[1][3]
    if optDeclId.isNone then none
    else
      let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore optDeclId[0]
      some id
  else
    none

/--
Update the name of the given declaration.
This function assumes `stx` is not a nameless instance.
-/
private def setDeclName (stx : Syntax) (name : Name) : Syntax :=
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 1 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][1] name
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    -- We never set the name of nameless instance declarations
    assert! !stx[1][3].isNone
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 3 <| stx[1][3].setArg 0 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][3][0] name
  else
    stx

/--
  Given declarations such as `@[...] def Foo.Bla.f ...` return `some (Foo.Bla, @[...] def f ...)`
  Remark: if the id starts with `_root_`, we return `none`.
-/
private def expandDeclNamespace? (stx : Syntax) : MacroM (Option (Name × Syntax)) := do
  let some name := getDeclName? stx | return none
  if (`_root_).isPrefixOf name then
    ensureValidNamespace (name.replacePrefix `_root_ Name.anonymous)
    return none
  let scpView := extractMacroScopes name
  match scpView.name with
  | .str .anonymous _ => return none
  | .str pre shortName => return some (pre, setDeclName stx { scpView with name := .mkSimple shortName }.review)
  | _ => return none

def elabAxiom (modifiers : Modifiers) (stx : Syntax) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  -- leading_parser "axiom " >> declId >> declSig
  let declId             := stx[1]
  let (binders, typeStx) := expandDeclSig stx[2]
  runTermElabM fun vars => do
    let scopeLevelNames ← Term.getLevelNames
    let ⟨shortName, declName, allUserLevelNames, docString?⟩ ← Term.expandDeclId (← getCurrNamespace) scopeLevelNames declId modifiers
    addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin declName modifiers.stx stx
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicit do
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicitForbiddenPred (fun n => shortName == n) do
    Term.withDeclName declName <| Term.withLevelNames allUserLevelNames <| Term.elabBinders binders.getArgs fun xs => do
      Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.beforeElaboration
      let type ← Term.elabType typeStx
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      let xs ← Term.addAutoBoundImplicits xs (declId.getTailPos? (canonicalOnly := true))
      let type ← instantiateMVars type
      let type ← mkForallFVars xs type
      let type ← mkForallFVars vars type (usedOnly := true)
      let type ← Term.levelMVarToParam type
      let usedParams  := collectLevelParams {} type |>.params
      match sortDeclLevelParams scopeLevelNames allUserLevelNames usedParams with
      | Except.error msg      => throwErrorAt stx msg
      | Except.ok levelParams =>
        let type ← instantiateMVars type
        let decl := Declaration.axiomDecl {
          name        := declName,
          levelParams := levelParams,
          type        := type,
          isUnsafe    := modifiers.isUnsafe
        }
        trace[Elab.axiom] "{declName} : {type}"
        Term.ensureNoUnassignedMVars decl
        addDecl decl

        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterTypeChecking
        if isExtern (← getEnv) declName then
          compileDecl decl
        if let some (doc, isVerso) := docString? then
          addDocStringOf isVerso declName binders doc
        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterCompilation
        withSaveInfoContext do  -- save new env with docstring and decl
          Term.addTermInfo' declId (← mkConstWithLevelParams declName) (isBinder := true)
        enableRealizationsForConst declName
open Lean.Parser.Command.InternalSyntax in
/--
Macro that expands a declaration with a complex name into an explicit `namespace` block.
Implementing this step as a macro means that reuse checking is handled by `elabCommand`.
 -/
@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.declaration]
def expandNamespacedDeclaration : Macro := fun stx => do
  match (← expandDeclNamespace? stx) with
  | some (ns, newStx) => do
    -- Limit ref variability for incrementality; see Note [Incremental Macros]
    let declTk := stx[1][0]
    let ns := mkIdentFrom declTk ns
    withRef declTk `(namespace $ns $endLocalScopeSyntax:command $(⟨newStx⟩) end $ns)
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_command_elab declaration, builtin_incremental]
def elabDeclaration : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  let modifiers : TSyntax ``Parser.Command.declModifiers := ⟨stx[0]⟩
  let decl     := stx[1]
  let declKind := decl.getKind
  if isDefLike decl then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef #[stx]
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    -- use hash of declaration name, if any, as stable quot context; `elabMutualDef` has its own
    -- handling
    withInitQuotContext (getDeclName? stx |>.map hash) do
    let mut modifiers ← elabModifiers modifiers
    if (← getScope).isMeta && modifiers.computeKind == .regular then
      modifiers := { modifiers with computeKind := .meta }
    withExporting (isExporting := modifiers.isInferredPublic (← getEnv)) do
      if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«axiom» then
        elabAxiom modifiers decl
      else if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«inductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«coinductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«structure» then
        elabInductive modifiers decl
      else
        throwError "unexpected declaration"

/-- Return true if all elements of the mutual-block are definitions/theorems/abbrevs. -/
private def isMutualDefLike (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx[1].getArgs.all fun elem =>
    let decl := elem[1]
    isDefLike decl

private def isMutualPreambleCommand (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  let k := stx.getKind
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.variable ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.universe ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.check ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.set_option ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.open

private partial def splitMutualPreamble (elems : Array Syntax) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
  let rec loop (i : Nat) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
    if h : i < elems.size then
      if isMutualPreambleCommand elems[i] then
        loop (i+1)
      else if i == 0 then
        none -- `mutual` block does not contain any preamble commands
      else
        some (elems[*...i], elems[i...elems.size])
    else
      none -- a `mutual` block containing only preamble commands is not a valid `mutual` block
  loop 0

/--
Find the common namespace for the given names.
Example:
```
findCommonPrefix [`Lean.Elab.eval, `Lean.mkConst, `Lean.Elab.Tactic.evalTactic]
-- `Lean
```
-/
def findCommonPrefix (ns : List Name) : Name :=
  match ns with
  | [] => .anonymous
  | n :: ns => go n ns
where
  go (n : Name) (ns : List Name) : Name :=
    match n with
    | .anonymous => .anonymous
    | _ => match ns with
      | [] => n
      | n' :: ns => go (findCommon n.components n'.components) ns
  findCommon (as bs : List Name) : Name :=
    match as, bs with
    | a :: as, b :: bs => if a == b then a ++ findCommon as bs else .anonymous
    | _, _ => .anonymous


@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualNamespace : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut nss := #[]
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    match (← expandDeclNamespace? elem) with
    | none        => Macro.throwUnsupported
    | some (n, _) => nss := nss.push n
  let common := findCommonPrefix nss.toList
  if common.isAnonymous then Macro.throwUnsupported
  let elemsNew ← stx[1].getArgs.mapM fun elem => do
    let some name := getDeclName? elem | unreachable!
    let view := extractMacroScopes name
    let nameNew := { view with name := view.name.replacePrefix common .anonymous }.review
    return setDeclName elem nameNew
  let ns := mkIdentFrom stx common
  let stxNew := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  `(namespace $ns $(⟨stxNew⟩) end $ns)

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualElement : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut elemsNew := #[]
  let mut modified := false
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    -- Don't trigger the `expandNamespacedDecl` macro, the namespace is handled by the mutual def
    -- elaborator directly instead
    if !elem.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.declaration then
      if let some elemNew ← expandMacro? elem then
        if elemNew.isOfKind nullKind then
          elemsNew := elemsNew ++ elemNew.getArgs
        else
          elemsNew := elemsNew.push elemNew
        modified := true
        continue
    elemsNew := elemsNew.push elem
  if modified then
    return stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  else
    Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualPreamble : Macro := fun stx =>
  match splitMutualPreamble stx[1].getArgs with
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported
  | some (preamble, rest) => do
    let secCmd    ← `(section)
    let newMutual := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode rest)
    let endCmd    ← `(end)
    return mkNullNode (#[secCmd] ++ preamble ++ #[newMutual] ++ #[endCmd])

@[builtin_command_elab «mutual», builtin_incremental]
def elabMutual : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  if isMutualDefLike stx then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef stx[1].getArgs
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    if ← isMutualInductive stx then
      elabMutualInductive stx[1].getArgs
    else
      throwError "invalid mutual block: either all elements of the block must be inductive/structure declarations, or they must all be definitions/theorems/abbrevs"

/- leading_parser "attribute " >> "[" >> sepBy1 (eraseAttr <|> Term.attrInstance) ", " >> "]" >> many1 ident -/
@[builtin_command_elab «attribute»] def elabAttr : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let mut attrInsts := #[]
  let mut toErase := #[]
  for attrKindStx in stx[2].getSepArgs do
    if attrKindStx.getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.eraseAttr then
      let attrName := attrKindStx[1].getId.eraseMacroScopes
      if isAttribute (← getEnv) attrName then
        toErase := toErase.push attrName
      else
        logErrorAt attrKindStx m!"unknown attribute [{attrName}]"
    else
      attrInsts := attrInsts.push attrKindStx
  let attrs ← elabAttrs attrInsts
  let idents := stx[4].getArgs
  for ident in idents do withRef ident <| liftTermElabM do
    /-
    HACK to allow `attribute` command to disable builtin simprocs.
    TODO: find a better solution. Example: have some "fake" declaration
    for builtin simprocs.
    -/
    let declNames ←
      try
        realizeGlobalConstWithInfos ident
      catch _ =>
        let name := ident.getId.eraseMacroScopes
        if (← Simp.isBuiltinSimproc name) then
          pure [name]
        else
          throwUnknownConstantAt ident name
    let declName ← ensureNonAmbiguous ident declNames
    withExporting (isExporting := !isPrivateName declName && attrs.any (·.kind != .local)) do
      recordExtraModUseFromDecl (isMeta := false) declName
    Term.applyAttributes declName attrs
    for attrName in toErase do
      Attribute.erase declName attrName
    if (← getEnv).isImportedConst declName then
      if let some attr := attrs.find? (·.kind != .local) then
        -- If an imported declaration is marked with a non-local attribute, Shake must be informed
        -- about this indirect reference
        recordIndirectModUse attr.name.toString declName

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.«initialize»] def elabInitialize : CommandElab
  | stx@`($declModifiers:declModifiers $kw:initializeKeyword $[$id? : $type? ←]? $doSeq) => do
    let attrId := mkIdentFrom stx <| if kw.raw[0].isToken "initialize" then `init else `builtin_init
    if let (some id, some type) := (id?, type?) then
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := stx[0]
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let defStx ← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$attrId:ident initFn, $(attrs?.getD ∅),*] $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? opaque $id : $type)
      let mut fullId := (← getCurrNamespace) ++ id.getId
      if vis?.any (·.raw.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.private) then
        fullId := mkPrivateName (← getEnv) fullId
      -- We need to add `id`'s ranges *before* elaborating `initFn` (and then `id` itself) as
      -- otherwise the info context created by `with_decl_name` will be incomplete and break the
      -- call hierarchy
      addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin fullId ⟨defStx.raw[0]⟩ defStx.raw[1]
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (!isPrivateName fullId)
      elabCommand (← `(
        $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO $type := with_decl_name% $(mkIdent fullId) do $doSeq
        $defStx:command))
    else
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(_)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := declModifiers
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let attrs := (attrs?.map (·.getElems)).getD #[]
      let attrs := attrs.push (← `(Lean.Parser.Term.attrInstance| $attrId:ident))
      -- `[builtin_init]` can be private as it is used for local codegen only but `[init]` must be
      -- available for the interpreter.
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (attrId.getId == `init)
      elabCommand (← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$[$attrs],*] $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO Unit := do $doSeq))
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

builtin_initialize
  registerTraceClass `Elab.axiom

end Lean.Elab.Command

/-- The **right-action** natural transformation of the coequalizer rows
(`def:relTensorActR`): `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`, whose
component at `U` is the objectwise right-action map
`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap` collapsing the middle ring factor through the scalar
action of `𝒪_X(U)` on `Γ(U,Q)`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ m ⊗ (s • n)`.  Naturality is the
compatibility of the module action with the restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`
on `Q`), checked on elementary tensors by `⊗`-induction. -/
noncomputable def relTensorActR (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
  naturality {U V} f := by
    have key :
        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↥(P.obj V)) (↥(Q.obj V))).comp
            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
              (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
                (objRestrict Q f)))
          = (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).comp
              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↥(P.obj U))
                (↥(Q.obj U))) := by
      apply TensorProduct.ext'
      intro m y
      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul s n =>
        change objRestrict P f m
              ⊗ₜ[ℤ] ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap s • objRestrict Q f n)
            = objRestrict P f m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f (s • n)
        congr 1
        rw [objRestrict_apply, objRestrict_apply]
        exact (PresheafOfModules.map_smul Q f s n).symm
      | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb, TensorProduct.tmul_add]
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    dsimp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf]
    ext z
    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply] using hz

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean

Iter-058 prover (mode: prove). Objectives: (a) 4 functoriality sorries
(473/474/490/491), (b) build `relTensorActL` (+`relTensorActR`), (c) if budget, start
`relTensorProj`.

## objRestrict_id / objRestrict_comp (NEW helpers, lines ~461/468)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** LinearMap-level identity/composition laws for the syntactic-carrier restriction
  `objRestrict P f`, proven by `ext x; simp [objRestrict_apply, Functor.map_id/map_comp,
  AddCommGrpCat.hom_id/hom_comp, AddMonoidHom.id_apply/coe_comp]`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED, axiom-clean. KEY GOTCHA: `P.presheaf` is **`Ab`/`AddCommGrpCat`-valued**,
  so `(P.presheaf.map f).hom` is an `AddMonoidHom` and the reductions need `AddCommGrpCat.hom_id`
  /`AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp` (NOT `ModuleCat.hom_id`/`hom_comp`).
- These two helpers are what make the triple-presheaf functoriality tractable (see below).

## relTensorDomainPresheaf map_id / map_comp (lines 473/474 → closed)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** `ext x; induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on`; tmul leaf
  `simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
  objRestrict_apply, Functor.map_id/map_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id/hom_comp,
  AddMonoidHom.id_apply/coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED, axiom-clean. The single (non-nested) `ℤ`-tensor: `map_tmul` fires fine
